title: Getting to Know You
description: Keep the chat going: work, the Cantonese compliment, and swapping WhatsApps
location: A social gathering, housewarming, or community event
context: The conversation is flowing. Now come the second-round questions: what you do, and the moment every learner gets - the compliment on your Cantonese. Deflect it like a local and you've passed the real test.
cultural_note: Any attempt at Cantonese earns you 你廣東話好叻喎 ("your Cantonese is so good"). The ritual is to deflect, never accept: 邊度係呀 ("not at all") is the local move. Taking the compliment at face value reads as odd. The deflection is not false modesty; it is an invitation to keep talking.

lines.speaker: them
lines.jyutping: nei5 zou6 mat1 je5 gung1 zok3 gaa3?
lines.english: What do you do for work?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 我係老師，喺國際學校教書。
lines.jyutping: ngo5 hai6 lou5 si1, hai2 gwok3 zai3 hok6 haau6 gaau3 syu1.
lines.english: I'm a teacher — I work at an international school.

lines.speaker: them
lines.cjk: 你廣東話好叻喎！學咗幾耐㗎？
lines.jyutping: nei5 gwong2 dung1 waa2 hou2 lek1 wo3! hok6 zo2 gei2 noi6 gaa3?
lines.english: Your Cantonese is great! How long have you been learning?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 邊度係呀！學咗半年咋，仲差好遠！
lines.jyutping: bin1 dou6 hai6 aa3! hok6 zo2 bun3 nin4 zaa3, zung6 caa1 hou2 jyun5!
lines.english: Not at all! I've only been learning six months. Still a long way to go!

lines.speaker: them
lines.jyutping: jau5 mou5 WhatsApp? bou2 ci4 lyun4 lok3 laa1!
lines.english: Got WhatsApp? Let's keep in touch!

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: jau5 aa3! hou2 gou1 hing3 sik1 dou2 nei5.
lines.english: Of course! Really glad to have met you.
